controls.objective: Changed behavior preserves explicit contracts, invariants, and error semantics.
controls.applicability: Applies to executable behavior, configuration interpretation, and generated runtime artifacts.
falsePositiveConditions: The challenged branch is unreachable in the bound subject.
A stronger caller or invariant proves the reported state cannot occur.
verificationRequirements: Cite the exact source range and violated contract.
Trace at least one reachable caller or entry point.
Review guards and associated tests before retaining the finding.
controls.remediationGuidance: Restore the explicit behavior or invariant and retain a deterministic regression check.

controls.objective: Reachable trust boundaries enforce authentication, authorization, validation, and secret handling.
controls.applicability: Applies wherever untrusted identities, input, data, files, processes, or networks cross a control boundary.
falsePositiveConditions: The input is not attacker-controlled in the bound deployment path.
An independently verified authorization or validation control blocks the failure path.
verificationRequirements: Identify preconditions, a reachable path, the affected asset, and mitigating controls before claiming exploitability.
Redact all secret-like values and retain no sensitive source preview.
Use CVSS only for a genuine reachable vulnerability.
controls.remediationGuidance: Place least-privilege authentication, authorization, validation, and secret controls at the reachable boundary.

controls.objective: Change ownership, complexity, generated sources, and operational comprehension remain defensible.
controls.applicability: Applies to maintained source, configuration, generated copies, and operational procedures.
falsePositiveConditions: The reported duplication is an intentionally generated or compatibility-owned copy.
The concern is a style preference without a concrete failure or ownership cost.
verificationRequirements: Identify the concrete comprehension, ownership, or change-amplification failure.
Check generated-source and compatibility constraints.
Exclude style-only preferences.
controls.remediationGuidance: Clarify ownership and reduce demonstrated change amplification without rewriting stable unrelated code.

controls.objective: Dependencies and component boundaries preserve the intended system design.
controls.applicability: Applies to component dependencies, trust boundaries, state ownership, and integration contracts.
falsePositiveConditions: The dependency is explicitly allowed by the bound architecture contract.
The apparent cycle exists only in build-time or generated metadata.
verificationRequirements: Map both sides of the boundary and the real dependency direction.
Identify the violated architectural decision or operational consequence.
Challenge the finding against compatibility and generated-copy constraints.
controls.remediationGuidance: Restore the intended dependency direction or document and verify an intentional boundary change.

controls.objective: Material performance claims are supported by retained reproducible benchmarks.
controls.applicability: Applies to latency, throughput, memory, storage, network, and scalability claims.
falsePositiveConditions: The measured change is within retained benchmark noise or uncertainty.
The benchmark does not represent the affected production path.
verificationRequirements: Retain a subject-bound reproducible benchmark for every percentage claim.
Record baseline, candidate, workload, environment, repetitions, and uncertainty outside sensitive output.
Downgrade unsupported percentage claims to unverified hypotheses.
controls.remediationGuidance: Optimize only the measured bottleneck and rerun the retained benchmark against the same subject and workload.

controls.objective: Dependencies, build inputs, and generated artifacts have bounded provenance and integrity.
controls.applicability: Applies to third-party dependencies, lockfiles, build tools, generated artifacts, and release inputs.
falsePositiveConditions: The dependency is not present in the resolved bound artifact.
A verified vendor backport or compensating integrity control removes the reported exposure.
verificationRequirements: Bind dependency evidence to an exact lockfile or artifact digest.
Request offline dependency resolution for curated local adapters and disclose that runner-level network isolation is not provided.
Treat stale or unavailable advisory data as incomplete coverage.
controls.remediationGuidance: Pin trusted inputs, update or remove affected dependencies, and retain provenance for rebuilt artifacts.

controls.objective: Critical behavior can be verified with deterministic, appropriately scoped evidence.
controls.applicability: Applies to changed or critical behavior and the seams needed to verify it deterministically.
falsePositiveConditions: Equivalent deterministic coverage exists at a stronger integration boundary.
The behavior is declarative and completely proven by a validated static contract.
verificationRequirements: Map the affected behavior to retained deterministic evidence.
Do not execute repository-controlled tests without exact subject approval.
Report unavailable or capped verification as incomplete coverage.
controls.remediationGuidance: Add the narrowest deterministic test seam that proves the contract without introducing production-only hooks.

controls.objective: Runtime observability, failure handling, rollout, and recovery are explicit and testable.
controls.applicability: Applies to deployable services, jobs, stateful processes, scheduled work, and operator-owned artifacts.
falsePositiveConditions: The component is not deployed or operated in the bound subject.
A verified platform control provides the reported missing recovery behavior.
verificationRequirements: Trace runtime failure detection, bounded retries, rollback, and recovery verification.
Identify the operator-visible signal and affected service objective.
Require current rollback context for release-profile completion.
controls.remediationGuidance: Add bounded failure handling, actionable telemetry, and a tested rollback or recovery procedure.

controls.objective: Persistence, concurrency, migration, and validation preserve data invariants.
controls.applicability: Applies to persisted, cached, queued, replicated, migrated, or concurrently updated data.
falsePositiveConditions: The state is ephemeral and cannot affect an external or retained invariant.
A verified transaction, constraint, or idempotency control blocks the failure path.
verificationRequirements: Name the invariant, write path, concurrency or migration preconditions, and failure outcome.
Review transactions, constraints, retries, and idempotency controls.
Bind evidence to the exact schema and source subject.
controls.remediationGuidance: Enforce the invariant at the authoritative write boundary and verify rollback, retry, and migration behavior.

controls.objective: Public interfaces and serialized contracts remain intentionally compatible.
controls.applicability: Applies to public APIs, CLIs, events, files, schemas, protocols, and cross-version persisted representations.
falsePositiveConditions: The interface is private to an atomically released and verified boundary.
The breaking change is explicitly versioned with a complete migration path.
verificationRequirements: Identify consumers, versioning rules, serialization defaults, and migration behavior.
Compare the exact before and after contract.
Review generated clients and compatibility-owned copies.
controls.remediationGuidance: Preserve the existing contract, introduce an explicit version, or provide a tested migration and deprecation path.

quick.description: Bounded change review without a whole-repository cleanliness claim.

standard.description: Changed modules, callers, dependencies, interfaces, tests, configuration, and applicable domains.

deep.description: Bounded whole-repository review with architecture, trust maps, challenge, and analyser evidence.

release.description: Exact release-range go/no-go review with complete, current, uncapped evidence.
